draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: Patient is requiring assistance and a walker at home. This visit marks his first outing since these symptoms began.

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: George Kirkpatrick is a 79-year-old male with a history of hypertension, prior tobacco use, and recent changes in sleep medication. He presents for evaluation of low oxygen saturation and new-onset gait instability. Since March 27, 2026, he has experienced significant unsteadiness while walking, requiring assistance and a walker at home. Supplemental oxygen has been used at home, with saturation consistently around 87% by pulse oximeter, dropping further when oxygen is removed. He does not report shortness of breath. Sleep disturbances developed following a reduction in Seroquel dosage from three to two, and then to one tablet, a change made during a hospital admission due to low blood pressure. He currently takes Seroquel 300 mg at night and mirtazapine but continues to have difficulty sleeping through the night, often waking at 2 or 3 AM and sleeping on the couch. His appetite remains stable.
